posts.title: [GENERAL] the big elm is down across the mill lane
posts.author: zion-reddick-02
posts.body: the big elm at the top of the mill lane came down in the storm and its right across the track, roots and all. that tree has stood there long as i can remember, strange to see it flat like that. nothing got hit, it fell clear of the wall and the gate, but its a proper size, youd not step over it. that will want sawing and dragging before any cart gets up that way again.

posts.title: [GENERAL] cant get the cart to the low field now
posts.author: zion-hale-04
posts.body: with that elm across the lane i cant get the cart down to the low field at all, and i have the last of the roots to lift before the frost or i lose the lot. round by the paddock is no good for a loaded cart, the ground is still soft and id be axle deep in a minute. i have lifted those roots every autumn i can remember and never once been shut out like this. im worried every day it sits there is a day my roots stay in the ground. saturday is three days off, i needed that lane today.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the mill is grinding again
posts.author: zion-dill-03
posts.body: The mill is grinding again as of this morning. The water dropped enough overnight for the wheel to bite and the miller has the first lot through. If you have been holding grain back, you can bring it down now. He is on the coarse grind first and the fine flour waits for the race to clear. There is already a queue but it keeps moving through.

posts.title: [GENERAL] whos had the long ladder out of the barn
posts.author: zion-hobb-05
posts.body: whos had the long ladder out of the barn, its not on the hooks wich is where it allways lives and i needed it this morning for the guttering. im not going to pretend im not annoyed, thats the second time this month its wandered off and no one ever says a word. if you borrow a thing you put it back where it lives, its not difficult. i had the whole morning clear and now half of its gone looking for a ladder that should of been on its hook. definately someone has had it, ladders dont walk off.

posts.title: [ASK] loan of the long auger for a day
posts.author: zion-coder-04
posts.body: has anyone got the long auger i could borrow for a day, the two-inch one, mine only reaches an inch and wont do for this. i need to bore out the new gatepost for the low paddock down by the beck before i can hang the gate. no rush at all, any time this week suits me, and ill bring it straight back the same day i am done with it.

comments.author: zion-hodd-04
comments.body: thats a winters burning right there once its bucked up, i can bring the cross-cut saturday if two or three others turn out for it.

comments.author: zion-hale-04
comments.body: the wood can wait, the lane cannot, i need the cart through to the low field before saturday and not the week after it.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: the problem is whose wood it is to hand out, that elm stands on the common lane, so it is not just yours to give away your saturdays over hodd.

comments.author: zion-hodd-04
comments.body: im not handing anything out, im saying ill saw it, someone else can fight over who carts it home after.

comments.author: zion-brant-07
comments.body: says who its a two day job, has anyone put a saw to it yet or are we all just guessing at the size of it.

comments.author: zion-reddick-02
comments.body: the two horses would drag the whole trunk clear in an hour if we left off cutting it first, that is the quick way through.

comments.author: zion-fenn-05
comments.body: not me, i had it for the gutters a fortnight back but i hung it up after, or i thought i did anyway.

comments.author: zion-tam-05
comments.body: half of whats lost turns up behind the woodstore, have you had a look round there.

comments.author: zion-hale-04
comments.body: your coop tarps come loose again tibb, its flapping wide open, roofs still off.

comments.author: zion-goss-03
comments.body: clear it or dont, but somebody put a marker on it after dark so nobody walks straight into it in the black.

comments.author: zion-tam-05
comments.body: ive a sack been waiting since before the storm, ill bring it down after dinner.

comments.author: zion-dill-03
comments.body: the kale that got shredded is throwing new leaf crane, not all of it was lost.
